code.description: Mermaid diagram code to analyze. Total request body must be 1 MiB or smaller. Supports suppression comments like `%% merm8-disable max-fanout` and `%% merm8-disable all`.
code.example:
graph TD
  A[Start] --> B[Process]
  B --> C[End]

config.description: Optional lint rule configuration. Canonical format is {"schema-version":"v1","rules":{...}}. Phase 1 accepts legacy flat/nested/snake_case config with deprecation warnings; Phase 2 rejects legacy config with 400 deprecated_config_format.

diagram-type.description: Normalized Mermaid diagram type (flowchart, sequence, class, er, state, unknown).

error.description: API-level error details for non-200 responses.

issues.description: Lint rule violations found in the diagram

lint-supported.description: Whether lint rules are currently implemented for the reported diagram type. Syntax-error responses use parser-detected type fallback when available.

metrics.description: Aggregate statistics about the diagram. Present for successful analyze responses, including syntax errors (zeroed counters with fallback diagram-type) and parsed but lint-unsupported families.

syntax-error.description: Syntax error details if `valid` is false. Null if valid.

valid.description: Whether the Mermaid code is syntactically valid and lint-supported (unsupported parsed families return valid=false).

warnings.description: Deprecation warnings emitted when legacy config keys/shapes are used.

path.description: JSON path to the invalid config field when config validation fails

supported.description: Supported values for unknown rule/option errors

column.description: 0-based column number where issue is located when known. Omitted when unknown.

context.description: Optional grouping context for node-scoped findings; omitted when no grouping applies.

fingerprint.description: Deterministic SHA-256 hash of the normalized issue signature for CI tracking and dedupe.

line.description: 1-based line number where issue is located when known. Omitted when unknown.

message.example: Node 'isolated' is not connected to the graph

rule-id.description: Identifier of the lint rule that triggered

subgraph-id.description: ID of the subgraph containing the affected node.

subgraph-label.description: Human-readable label of the subgraph containing the affected node.

diagram-type.description: Diagram type copied from parsed metadata

direction.description: Diagram direction when provided by parser

disconnected-node-count.description: Number of nodes with neither incoming nor outgoing edges

duplicate-node-count.description: Number of duplicate node declarations beyond first occurrences

edge-count.description: Total number of edges (connections) in the diagram

issue-counts.description: Issue count aggregations by severity and rule ID

max-fanin.description: Maximum number of incoming edges to any single node

max-fanout.description: Maximum number of outgoing edges from any single node

node-count.description: Total number of nodes in the diagram

limit.description: Maximum allowed depth for root-to-leaf traversals.

severity.description: Severity assigned to emitted issues (case-insensitive; surrounding whitespace ignored).

limit.description: Maximum allowed outgoing edges per node.

severity.description: Severity assigned to emitted issues (case-insensitive; surrounding whitespace ignored).

allow-self-loop.description: Allow single-node self-loop cycles without emitting issues.

SARIFReport.description: Severity mapping to SARIF level: error->error, warning/warn->warning, info->note.

line.description: 1-based line number where the error occurred

message.description: Error message from the Mermaid parser

info.description:
A deterministic Mermaid static analysis engine that validates and lints Mermaid diagrams.

- Accepts Mermaid code via HTTP POST
- Validates syntax using the official Mermaid parser
- Returns structured syntax errors if invalid
- Runs deterministic lint rules on valid diagrams
- Returns structured lint results with metrics
info.title: merm8 - Mermaid Lint API

post.description:
Validates Mermaid code syntax and runs deterministic lint rules.

Maximum request body size is 1 MiB. Oversized payloads return HTTP 413.

Operational note: tune `PARSER_CONCURRENCY_LIMIT` and `PARSER_MAX_OLD_SPACE_MB` (documented in API_GUIDE.md under Operational environment variables). When parser concurrency is exhausted, `/analyze` returns HTTP 503 with `error.code=server_busy`; parser subprocess heap is capped with Node `--max-old-space-size`.

Supports source comment suppressions such as `%% merm8-disable <rule-id>`, `%% merm8-disable all`, and `%% merm8-disable-next-line <rule-id>`.

Returns syntax errors as semantic responses (`valid=false`) if parsing fails, or lint results if parsing succeeds.

Parser infrastructure failures return machine-readable API error codes:
- `parser_subprocess_error`
- `parser_decode_error`
- `parser_contract_violation`
- `parser_timeout` (HTTP 504)

graph LR
  A --> B
  B --> C

graph LR
  A --> B
  B --> C

graph LR
  A --> B
  B --> C

issues.message: Node 'D' is not connected to the graph
Node 'A' has fanout 6, exceeding limit of 5

200.description: Analysis completed. For HTTP 200: `error` is always null, `issues` is always present as an array, and `syntax-error` is populated only for parser syntax failures (otherwise null).

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

400.description: Bad request (invalid JSON, missing required field, or invalid config). For non-200 API failures, `valid=false`, `syntax-error=null`, `issues=[]`, and `error` is populated.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

413.description: Request entity too large (body exceeds 1 MiB). For non-200 API failures, `error` is populated, `syntax-error` is null, and `issues` is an empty array.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

429.description: Rate limited. For non-200 API failures, `error` is populated, `syntax-error` is null, and `issues` is an empty array.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

500.description: Internal server error. For non-200 API failures, `error` is populated, `syntax-error` is null, and `issues` is an empty array.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

error.message: parser concurrency limit reached; try again

503.description: Service unavailable. For non-200 API failures, `error` is populated, `syntax-error` is null, and `issues` is an empty array.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current time window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

error.message: parser timed out while validating Mermaid code

504.description: Parser timeout. For non-200 API failures, `error` is populated, `syntax-error` is null, and `issues` is an empty array.

get.description: Serve Swagger UI for interactive API exploration and testing
Canonical v1 process liveness-only probe. Returns process liveness status (dependency checks are handled by /ready).
Returns internal JSON counters for analyze outcomes: valid_success, syntax_error, and parser failure categories timeout/subprocess/decode/contract/internal.
Dependency/readiness-only probe. Returns readiness status for required dependencies (including parser checks when available) and may return 503 when not ready.
Returns centralized metadata for all built-in rules, including defaults and configurable options.
Returns a JSON Schema generated from the rule config registry. The schema validates only the canonical versioned config payload format.
get.summary: Get JSON Schema for lint rule configuration

get.description: Returns the OpenAPI 3.0 specification for this API in JSON format
